title: Carlos Tuimavave

{{short description|Samoa international rugby league footballer}} {{Use dmy dates|date=April 2022}} {{EngvarB|date=April 2022}} {{Infobox rugby league biography |name = Carlos Tuimavave |fullname = Carlos Chanel Courtney Tuimavave[https://find-and-update.company-information.service.gov.uk/officers/ZTIOWqPZH3T1CT8cXN2Az5H9Fbs/appointments Companies House] |image = Carlos Tuimavave Warriors.jpg |imagesize = 285 |caption = |birth_date = {{birth date and age|df=y|1992|1|10}} |birth_place = [[Auckland, New Zealand]] |height = {{convert|1.85|m|ftin|abbr=on|order=flip}} |weight = {{convert|94|kg|stlb|abbr=on|order=flip}} |position = {{rlp|CE|SO}} |club1 = {{nowrap|[[New Zealand Warriors]]}} |year1start = 2012 |year1end = 14 |appearances1 = 9 |tries1 = 2 |goals1 = 0 |fieldgoals1 = 0 |points1 = 8 |club2 = [[Newcastle Knights]] |year2start = 2015 |year2end = |appearances2 = 5 |tries2 = 1 |goals2 = 0 |fieldgoals2 = 0 |points2 = 4 |club3 = [[Hull F.C.]] |year3start = 2016 |year3end = 24 |appearances3 = 182 |tries3 = 58 |goals3 = 0 |fieldgoals3 = 0 |points3 = 232 | club4 = {{nowrap|[[Featherstone Rovers]]}} | year4start = 2025 | year4end = | appearances4 = 5 | tries4 = 2 | goals4 = 0 | fieldgoals4 = 0 | points4 = 8 |teamA = [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] |yearAstart = 2013 |yearAend = 15 |appearancesA = 3 |triesA = 1 |goalsA = 0 |fieldgoalsA = 0 |pointsA = 4 |retired = |source = {{Cite web|last=|first=|title=Carlos Tuimavave|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/players/|access-date=2021-05-19|website=LoveRugbyLeague|language=en-GB|archive-date=3 April 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190403181023/https://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/players/|url-status=dead}}{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/carlos-tuimavave/summary.html|title=Carlos Tuimavave - Career Stats & Summary|publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=2015-10-15}} | updated = {{date|{{REVISIONTIMESTAMP}}|DMY}} | module = {{infobox person | embed = yes | father = | mother = | spouse = | education = [[Sacred Heart College, Auckland]] | alma_mater = | relatives = [[Evarn Tuimavave]] (cousin)
[[Antonio Winterstein]] (cousin)
[[Tony Tuimavave]] (uncle)
[[Paddy Tuimavave]] (uncle)
[[Mike Setefano]] (step-father) }} }} '''Carlos Tuimavave''' (born 10 January 1992) is a former [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|so}} for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship]]. He previously played for [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]], captaining the side in the [[2023 Super League season|2023 season]], and the [[New Zealand Warriors]] and the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]]. ==Background== Tuimavave was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand, and is of [[Samoans|Samoan]] and [[Europe]]an descent.{{cite web |url=https://www.hullfc.com/blog/2022/11/16/tuimavave-on-samoan-heritage-ahead-of-world-cup-final/ |title=Tuimavave On Samoan Heritage Ahead Of World Cup Final |date=16 November 2022 |publisher=Hull & Proud |access-date=8 February 2023}} He played his junior football for the [[Mount Wellington Warriors]] while attending [[St. Patricks Catholic School]] and [[Sacred Heart College, Auckland|Sacred Heart College]], both in east Auckland. Tuimavave is a first cousin of former [[New Zealand Warriors]], [[Newcastle Knights]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] player, [[Evarn Tuimavave]], [[Adam Tuimavave- Gerrard]] who also played [[New Zealand Warriors]] and, [[Antonio Winterstein]] who played for [[Brisbane Broncos]] and [[The Cowboys]]. Tuimavave and cousins mentioned above are nephew’s of former [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] and [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoan]] internationals, [[Tony Tuimavave]] and [[Paddy Tuimavave]].{{cite web|author=Aaron Lawton|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/6867558/Rugby-league-family-ties-boost-fledglings|title=Rugby, league family ties boost fledglings|publisher=Stuff.co.nz|date=2012-05-05|access-date=2015-06-27}} [[Mike Setefano]] a known player in the league scene is Carlos Tuimavave’s step father. Setefano played for the [[Auckland City Vulcans]] in the 1994 Lion Red Cup, and toured New Zealand with [[Western Samoa]] in 1994. In 1995 he joined the [[North Harbour Sea Eagles]], and was part of the team that won the [[1995 Lion Red Cup]]. He was part of the Western Samoa [[1992 Pacific Cup (rugby league)#Squads|1992 Pacific Cup]], and [[Pacific Challenge#Squads|1996 Pacific Challenge sides]]. ==Playing career== ===Early career=== When he was 16, Tuimavave moved to [[Sydney|Sydney, Australia]] after being signed by the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]].{{cite web|author=Ben Stanley|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/home-is-sweet-for-carlos-and-so-is-his-senior-debut-20120804-23mb6.html|title=Home is sweet for Carlos, and so is his senior debut|publisher=Smh.com.au|date=2012-08-04|access-date=2015-06-27}} Becoming homesick, he was offered the chance to join the [[New Zealand Warriors]] [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] squad in 2010. [[File:Carlos Tuimavave.jpg|thumb|right|Tuimavave playing for the Junior Warriors in 2011]] From 2010 to 2012, he played for the Warriors' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team,{{cite web|url=http://rleague.com/players/Carlos-Tuimavave_2873/Stats/|title=has been shutdown|publisher=Rleague.com|access-date=2015-05-31|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150622195710/http://rleague.com/players/Carlos-Tuimavave_2873/Stats/|archive-date=2015-06-22}} playing in both the Warriors' 2010 and 2011 Grand Final victories. Forming a halves partnership with [[Shaun Johnson]], Tuimavave was named the Man of the Match in the 2010 Grand Final and also won the Warriors' Young [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] Player of the Year award.{{cite web|date=2012|title=Carlos Tuimavave|url=http://www.warriors.co.nz/playerprofiledisplay/Warriors/Carlos-Tuimavave/2759|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120711041235/http://www.warriors.co.nz/playerprofiledisplay/Warriors/Carlos-Tuimavave/2759|archive-date=11 July 2012|access-date=31 July 2012}} In October 2010, he played for the [[Junior Kiwis]] against the [[Junior Kangaroos]].{{cite web|url=http://www.nzrl.co.nz/news-archive/junior-kiwis-named-.aspx|title=NZRL|publisher=NZRL.co.nz|date=2010-10-06|access-date=2015-06-27|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://archive.today/20120907181916/http://www.nzrl.co.nz/news-archive/junior-kiwis-named-.aspx|archive-date=2012-09-07}}{{cite web|date=2 October 2012|title=Junior Kiwis squad announced|url=http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=45763|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130130142458/http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=45763|archive-date=30 January 2013|access-date=17 March 2014}} On 30 August 2011, he was named at five-eighth in the 2011 NYC Team of the Year.{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/toyota-cup-team-of-the-year/tabid/10874/newsid/64496/default.aspx|title=Toyota Cup Team of the Year|publisher=NRL.com|date=2011-08-30|access-date=2015-06-27|archive-date=11 May 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150511103631/http://www.nrl.com/toyota-cup-team-of-the-year/tabid/10874/newsid/64496/default.aspx|url-status=dead}} On 16 October 2011, he captained the Junior Kiwis.{{cite web|url=http://www.nzrl.co.nz/news-archive/junior-kiwis-fielding-huge-line-up-.aspx|title=NZRL|publisher=NZRL.co.nz|date=2011-10-16|access-date=2015-06-27|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://archive.today/20150404231836/http://www.nzrl.co.nz/news-archive/junior-kiwis-fielding-huge-line-up-.aspx|archive-date=2015-04-04}} In 2012, he became the club's sixth player to appear in 50 [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] matches.{{cite web|url=http://www.voxy.co.nz/sport/no-changes-junior-warriors/5/129578|title=No changes for Junior Warriors|publisher=Voxy.co.nz|date=2012-07-21|access-date=2015-06-27}} ===2012=== In Round 22 of the [[2012 NRL season]], Tuimavave made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Warriors against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], becoming the [[List of New Zealand Warriors players|177th player]] to play for the Warriors.{{cite web|title=Milestones and another debutant for warriors|url=https://nzwarriors.com/threads/milestones-and-another-debutant-on-sunday.4722/|url-status=live|access-date=31 July 2012|website=nzwarriors.com|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210225100712/https://nzwarriors.com/threads/milestones-and-another-debutant-on-sunday.4722/ |archive-date=2021-02-25 }}{{cbignore|bot=medic}} He came on the field after 20 minutes filling in for the injured five-eighth, [[James Maloney (rugby league)|James Maloney]]. On 21 August 2012, he was named at five-eighth in the 2012 NYC Team of the Year.{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/toyota-cup-team-of-the-year/tabid/10874/newsid/69388/default.aspx|title=Toyota Cup Team of the Year|publisher=NRL.com|date=2012-08-21|access-date=2015-04-05}} On 13 October 2012, he played for the Junior Kiwis against the Junior Kangaroos for the third time.{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsportspulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=1-4977-0-0-0&sID=64815&news_task=DETAIL&articleID=20791193|title=Junior Kiwis Squad announced - Canterbury Rugby League|publisher=Foxsportspulse.com|date=2012-10-02|access-date=2015-06-27}} At the end of 2012, he was named one of the top ten young players of 2012 in an article by Lifestyle Uncut.{{cite web|url=http://www.lifestyleuncut.com/sport/top-10-youngsters-from-the-nrl-youd-like-to-see-in-super-league.html|title=The Top 10 Youngsters From The NRL You'd Like To See In Super League by Chris Taylor of Lifestyle Uncut|publisher=Lifestyleuncut.com|date=2012-09-23|access-date=2015-05-31|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131203060554/http://www.lifestyleuncut.com/sport/top-10-youngsters-from-the-nrl-youd-like-to-see-in-super-league.html|archive-date=2013-12-03}} ===2013=== In 2013, Tuimavave elected to play for [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] at senior level and made his international debut in their Pacific Rugby League International clash against [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] in Penrith.{{cite news |title=Samoa calls on former Kiwis to help in test bid |work=Dominion-Post |page=B13 |date=16 April 2013 |via=EBSCOHost}} ===2014=== In May 2014, Tuimavave played for Samoa in the 2014 Pacific Rugby League International. He scored a try in Samoa's 32-16 test-match win. In June 2014, he signed a 2-year contract with the [[Newcastle Knights]] starting in 2015.{{cite web|author=David Long|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/10211362/Carlos-Tuimavave-is-off-to-Newcastle-Knights|title=Carlos Tuimavave is off to Newcastle Knights|publisher=Stuff.co.nz|date=2014-06-29|access-date=2015-05-31}} On 8 September 2014, he was named in the Samoa train-on squad for the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]],{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/samao-train-on-squad-announced-14837/|title=Samoa train on squad announced | NRL|publisher=Zerotackle.com|access-date=2015-05-31|archive-date=14 October 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141014081012/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/samao-train-on-squad-announced-14837/|url-status=dead}} but didn't make the final 24-man squad. ===2015=== On 2 May, Tuimavave played for Samoa against Tonga in the 2015 [[Polynesian Cup]].{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/85557/default.aspx|title=Representative Round team lists|publisher=NRL.com|date=2015-05-03|access-date=2015-05-31}} In Round 12 of the [[2015 NRL season]], he made his Knights debut against his former club, the [[New Zealand Warriors]].{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2015/05/31/late_mail_warriors_v.html|title=Late Mail: Warriors v Knights - Knights|date=31 May 2015 |publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2015-05-31}} On 25 July, it was confirmed that Tuimavave would be heading off to play in England next season for [[Super League]] side [[Hull F.C.]]{{cite web|url=http://superleague.co.uk/article/32780/tuimavave-heading-to-hull|title=Tuimavave heading to Hull|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=2015-07-25|access-date=2015-07-25}} On 27 September, he played in the Knights' [[2015 New South Wales Cup#Chart|2015 New South Wales Cup Grand Final]] win over the [[Wyong Roos]].{{cite web|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2015/09/22/vb_nsw_cup_grand_fin.html|title=VB NSW Cup Grand Final Team List|date=22 September 2015 |publisher=NSWRL|access-date=2015-10-15}}{{cite web|author=Brett Keeble|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/newcastle-knights/newcastle-knights-extend-clint-newtons-career-with-nsw-cup-win-over-wyong-roos-20150927-gjw2gs.html|title=Newcastle Knights extend Clint Newton's career with NSW Cup win over Wyong Roos|date=27 September 2015 |publisher=Smh.com.au|access-date=2015-10-15}} ===2016=== Tuimavave was given the number 3 shirt for the Hull F.C. side in the 2016 season. He began his Hull FC career in a 60-20 friendly win over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].{{cite web|url=http://hullfc.com/article/43688/match-report-hull-fc-60-20-hull-kingston-rovers|title=The Official Hull FC Website|publisher=Hullfc.com|access-date=2016-02-25}} On 27 August, he would go on to make history as he played in the [[Challenge Cup]] Final for Hull F.C. against [[Warrington Wolves]]. Hull, having never won at [[Wembley Stadium]] in 8 attempts had to come back from 10-0 down with 20 minutes to go to win the game 12-10, giving him his first major trophy and going down in the history books as part of the first Hull team to win at Wembley.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}} ===2017=== On 26 August 2017, Tuimavave won the [[2017 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]] for a second year in a row in Hull's 18-14 win over the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|publisher=Mirror|date=26 August 2017|access-date=6 November 2019}} ===2018=== Tuimavave played 18 games in the [[2018 Super League season]] as Hull F.C. finished sixth on the table.{{citation needed|date=January 2023}} ===2019=== Tuimavave played 23 games for Hull F.C. in the [[2019 Super League season]] as the club finished sixth and missed the playoffs.{{citation needed|date=January 2023}} ===2020=== Tuimavave played 17 games for Hull F.C. in the [[2020 Super League season]] as the club got to within one game of the grand final.{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-hull-fc-highlights-4716172#964303/|title=Wigan Warriors v Hull FC highlights as FC struggle to find play-off form in one-sided defeat|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|date=19 November 2020 }} At the end of the 2020 season, Tuimavave won Hull FC's Player of the Year award, as well as scooping the Try of the Season for his effort against St Helens in Round 3. ===2021=== Tuimavave played 20 games for Hull F.C. and scored nine tries in the 2021 Super League season which saw the club finish 8th on the table and miss out on the playoffs.{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12415217/terry-oconnors-2021-betfred-super-league-season-review/|title=Terry O'Connor's 2021 Betfred Super League season review|website=www.skysports.com}} ===2022=== Tuimavave played a total of eight games for Hull F.C. in the 2022 Super League season which saw the club finish 9th on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/hull-fcs-manu-mau-makes-big-super-league-move-55934/|title=Hull FC's Manu Ma'u makes big Super League move|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com|date=12 July 2022 }} ===2023=== Tuimavave played a total of 16 games for Hull F.C. for in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished 10th on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429/|title=Hull FC highs and lows as mid-season resurgence not enough to prevent drop-off|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|access-date=4 October 2023|archive-date=4 October 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231004073738/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429|url-status=dead}} ===2024=== During the 2024 Super League season, Tuimavave announced it would be his last with Hull F.C. He played a total of 15 games for the club as they finished second last on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/special-place-carlos-tuimavave-shares-9635584/|title='Special place' Carlos Tuimavave shares Hull FC farewell message ahead of new chapter|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}} On 16 November 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a two-year deal.{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=16 Nov 2024|author=Drew Darbyshire|title=Carlos Tuimavave's next move confirmed after Hull FC exit with opportunity 'too good to refuse'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/carlos-tuimavave-featherstone-rovers-move-confirmed-after-hull-fc-exit}} ===2026=== On 13 April 2026 he announced his retirement as a professional{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=13 Apr 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Hull FC cult hero hangs up boots as retirement confirmed in emotional social media post|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/carlos-tuimavave-retires-april-2026}} == Statistics == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Year !Team !Games !Tries !Pts |- | align=center|2012 | rowspan="3"
